Patient-derived immunocompetent cultures reveal personalized immunotherapies and new treatment options for therapy resistant patients with high-grade serous ovarian cancer

Anniina, Färkkilä, MD PhD 
Assistant Professor, Specialist in Gynecology
University of Helsinki and Helsinki University Hospital, Helsinki, Finland

Disclosure Information Ovarian Cancer October 5-7, 2023 | Westin Seaport Boston | Boston, MA

Anniina Färkkilä
I have the following relevant financial relationships to disclose:
 Consultant for: GSK
 Speaker’s Bureau for: GSK, Astra Zeneca
 Grant/Research support from: GSK, Astra Zeneca

High-Grade Serous Ovarian Carcinoma (HGSC)

Most common (~70%) and lethal subtype of ovarian cancers
 ~ 80% of the patients exhibit omental metastasis
HGSC harbor genotype-specific tumor microenvironment (TME)
BRCA1/2 mut tumors exhibit increased immunosurveillance compared to HR wt
Enhanced spatial interactions between proliferating epithelial cells and T cells in BRCA1/2 mut tumors (Launonen et al., 2022) 
Treatment responses
PARPi effective against HRD tumors
Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICI) response rate is 8-10% (Zhu et al., 2021)
Better response to combination of PARPi plus anti-PD-1 in HRD patients (Färkkilä et al., 2020)

Workflow for testing novel combination therapies on HGSC patient-derived immunocompetent cultures

Effective killing dose for cancer cells (ED75): proportion of images per treatment in which at least 75% of cancer cells are dead, normalized to control
2. Cancer cell-specific death: (CK7+ dead) - (CD45+ dead)) / all cells per treatment normalized to control

DO NOT POST

DO NOT POST

Summary

Novel patient-derived OmGel matrix significantly improves the growth of HGSC patient-derived cultures compared to conventional matrix

iPDCs maintain source tumor-derived lymphocytes and myeloid cells critical for eliciting response to immunotherapy combinations 

iPDCs exhibit tumor genotype-specific response to Olaparib and anti-PD1 treatments, and recapitulate clinical response to PARPi or chemotherapy

 The presence of physiologically relevant patient-derived matrix combined with patient-specific tumor infiltrating immune cells makes iPDCs a reliable pre-clinical model for high throughput testing of novel immuno-oncology drugs and combination therapies
